BASELINE_VOTE5_DISJOINT.notes: Validates plurality voting structurally on disjoint seed pool

BASELINE_VOTE5_H128.notes: Capacity h=128; wins fold-6 (+10.70) and DQN-test (+10.59); 5/6 folds

BASELINE_VOTE5_H256.notes: Capacity h=256; best WF aggregate but weak fold-6 (+0.41)

VOTE5_V6.notes: State v6 (direction probs in state) — net negative, abandoned

VOTE5_DOUBLE.notes: Double DQN; weak val, modest WF

VOTE5_DUELING.notes: Dueling DQN alone; worst test of any variant

BASELINE_VOTE5_DD.notes: Double_Dueling; best val (+6.12) of any K=5; regularized alternative

FEE4_p001.notes: Fee-aware retrain (fee=0.0004 in reward, penalty=0.001); best val under fee at -1.83 @ 4.5bp

VOTE10_DD_seed1.notes: Z1.3 DD disjoint pool member; tag was VOTE10_DD but ensemble role is VOTE5_DD_DISJOINT

VOTE10_DD_seed13.notes: Z1.3 DD disjoint pool member; tag was VOTE10_DD but ensemble role is VOTE5_DD_DISJOINT

VOTE10_DD_seed77.notes: Z1.3 DD disjoint pool member; tag was VOTE10_DD but ensemble role is VOTE5_DD_DISJOINT

VOTE5_H256_DD.notes: Z1.1 winner — H256 capacity + DD regularization stack. Test +9.01 is highest in project. Fold-6 +8.23 (recovered from H256's +0.41).

VOTE5_DD_DISJOINT.notes: Z1.3 — disjoint validation of DD ensemble. Different val/WF profile than orig BASELINE_VOTE5_DD (+6.80 WF) — DD result is seed-sensitive on those metrics but 6/6 folds preserved.

VOTE5_H128_DISJOINT.notes: Z1.4 H128 disjoint — REVEALS H128 IS SEED-LUCK. Original H128 fold-6 +10.70 collapses to -4.82 in disjoint. Drop H128 from baseline rotation.

VOTE5_H256_DISJOINT.notes: Z1.4 H256 disjoint — H256 reproduces well. Mean of orig+disjoint WF ≈ +10.86. Disjoint actually has nicer val (+5.66) and fold-6 (+6.75).

VOTE10_VANILLA.notes: Z1.2 — K=10 vanilla VOTE10. NEGATIVE: WF -0.75 vs K=5, val -4.06. K=10 ties produce more NO_TRADE; trade count drops 1122 → 965.

VOTE5_v7pa_H256_DD_seed42.notes: Z2 Step 2 member — v7_pa price-action state

VOTE5_v7pa_H256_DD_seed7.notes: Z2 Step 2 member — v7_pa price-action state

VOTE5_v7pa_H256_DD_seed99.notes: Z2 Step 2 member — v7_pa price-action state

VOTE5_v8_H256_DD_seed42.notes: Z3 Step 4 member — v8_s11s13 (12 actions: S11+S13 added)

VOTE5_v8_H256_DD_seed7.notes: Z3 Step 4 member — v8_s11s13 (12 actions: S11+S13 added)

VOTE5_v8_H256_DD_seed123.notes: Z3 Step 4 member — v8_s11s13 (12 actions: S11+S13 added)

VOTE5_v8_H256_DD_seed0.notes: Z3 Step 4 member — v8_s11s13 (12 actions: S11+S13 added)

VOTE5_v8_H256_DD_seed99.notes: Z3 Step 4 member — v8_s11s13 (12 actions: S11+S13 added)

VOTE5_v7pa_H256_DD.notes: Z2 Step 2 — NEGATIVE. Price-action context features hurt policy. WF -1.87 vs baseline, val collapses to -1.49, fold-5 negative.

VOTE5_v7basis_H256_DD.notes: Z2 Step 3 — PARTIAL. Val resilience hypothesis CONFIRMED (val +3.21 → +6.05). WF +0.61. But test (-6.11) and fold-6 (-5.92) regressed. Keep as val-robust alternative baseline.

VOTE5_v8_H256_DD.notes: Z3 Step 4 — WIN. New primary baseline. S11+S13 added to action space (10→12). WF +1.02 vs VOTE5_H256_DD. Val +3.46. Test dropped (-4.57) but WF aggregate higher and 6/6 folds.

VOTE5_v9_H256_DD_seed42.notes: Z2/Z3 Step 5 member — v9 combined (v7_basis state + v8 action space)

VOTE5_v9_H256_DD_seed7.notes: Z2/Z3 Step 5 member — v9 combined (v7_basis state + v8 action space)

VOTE5_v9_H256_DD_seed123.notes: Z2/Z3 Step 5 member — v9 combined (v7_basis state + v8 action space)

VOTE5_v9_H256_DD_seed0.notes: Z2/Z3 Step 5 member — v9 combined (v7_basis state + v8 action space)

VOTE5_v9_H256_DD_seed99.notes: Z2/Z3 Step 5 member — v9 combined (v7_basis state + v8 action space)

VOTE5_v9_H256_DD.notes: Z2/Z3 Step 5 — DOES NOT COMPOSE. Combined v7_basis (Step 3) state + v8 (Step 4) action space lost WF (-1.60 vs Step 4) AND val (-2.27). Features overlap: state has basis context AND S11 uses basis. Retained as fold-6-robust variant (fold-6 +6.95, best in v8-family) and best test in v8-family (+7.04).

VOTE5_v8_DISJOINT_H256_DD_seed1.notes: Path A3 — disjoint validation of v8 (seeds 1/13/25/50/77)

VOTE5_v8_DISJOINT_H256_DD_seed13.notes: Path A3 — disjoint validation of v8 (seeds 1/13/25/50/77)

VOTE5_v8_DISJOINT_H256_DD_seed25.notes: Path A3 — disjoint validation of v8 (seeds 1/13/25/50/77)

VOTE5_v8_DISJOINT_H256_DD_seed50.notes: Path A3 — disjoint validation of v8 (seeds 1/13/25/50/77)

VOTE5_v8_DISJOINT_H256_DD_seed77.notes: Path A3 — disjoint validation of v8 (seeds 1/13/25/50/77)

VOTE5_v8_DISJOINT_H256_DD.notes: A3 disjoint validation of VOTE5_v8. Reveals v8 has SEED-POOL VARIANCE: orig val +6.67 vs disjoint val -2.12. Mean WF across pools: +10.49. 6/6 folds positive in both pools — structural improvement is real but smaller than headline.

VOTE5_v8_CURR_H256_DD.notes: C1/Z4.3 — NEGATIVE. Curriculum learning (calm→trends→all) catastrophically hurt WF (-8.72 vs v8). Fold-6 target benefit didn't materialize (-0.35). Hypothesis: training only on calm bars early biases the buffer; rare regimes (ranging, chop) get insufficient trajectory exposure in phase 3.

DISTILL_v8_seed42.notes: C2 — single net distilled from VOTE5_v8_H256_DD plurality labels. Strongest individual seed.

DISTILL_v8_seed123.notes: C2 distilled student, seed 123 (best test Sharpe)

DISTILL_v8_VOTE5.notes: C2 — 5-seed plurality of distilled students. UNDERPERFORMS individual seeds because correlated students vote together (no diversity benefit). Single distilled net beats voting of distilled nets.

VOTE5_v8_H256_DD_eth_seed42.notes: Z2.1 cross-asset — ETH. Single-seed greedy val is negative; ensemble plurality is positive.

VOTE5_v8_H256_DD_eth.notes: Z2.1 — ETH cross-asset. WF +7.22, val +5.57, test -0.09. Strategy stack transfers at ensemble level (5/6 folds positive) but loses on test (most recent period). Single-seed greedy training all negative; plurality vote recovers positive Sharpe via defensive filtering.

VOTE5_v8_H256_DD_sol.notes: Z2.1 — SOL cross-asset. WF +8.24, val +4.16, test +2.19, 6/6 folds positive. Best cross-asset transfer of the two non-BTC tickers.

QRDQN_v8_seed42.notes: Z4.4 — quantile-regression DQN with CVaR-0.3 action selection. Single-seed val +6.39.

QRDQN_v8_seed0.notes: Z4.4 QR-DQN. Best test +7.95 at CVaR=0.3.

QRDQN_v8_VOTE5.notes: Z4.4 NEGATIVE-LEAN. Plurality vote with CVaR-0.3 action selection. WF +11.58, val +4.88, test +5.12 — does NOT beat VOTE5_v8 (WF +12.07) or DISTILL_v8_seed42 (val +10.41, test +9.35). Best single seed (s=0) hits test +7.95 but worse val.

XFMR_v8_seed0.notes: Z4.2 Transformer-DQN. Project-record single-seed greedy val Sharpe (+11.11). Outlier — plurality voting dilutes it.

XFMR_v8_VOTE5.notes: Z4.2 NEGATIVE. Plurality vote. WF +6.35, val +4.88, test +5.95 — underperforms VOTE5_v8_H256_DD (WF +12.07) by ~5.7 Sharpe and DISTILL_v8_seed42 (test +9.35). Multi-head attention's pairwise-interaction prior doesn't match this curated-feature state vector.

DISTILL_XFMR_v8_seed42.notes: Z4.2 follow-on. NEGATIVE — distilled XFMR ensemble. Val +9.41 / test +2.83 — wide spread vs canonical DISTILL_v8_seed42 (val +10.41 / test +9.35). One fold flips negative (5/6). Student inherits weak teacher signal.
